prerequisites: A Railz (FIS Accounting Data as a Service) account. Sign up at https://dashboard.railz.ai/signup.
An API key generated in the Railz Dashboard, which provides the client_id and the secret_key (shown only once at creation).
A start_date value to define the earliest point from which to sync records.

authMethods.name: API key (client_id + secret_key)
authMethods.summary: Railz authenticates with a client_id and a secret_key generated from the Dashboard. These are combined using HTTP Basic Authentication to call the Get Access Token endpoint, which returns a short-lived bearer access_token (valid 60 minutes) used as Authorization: Bearer for API requests. The connector stores the client_id and the secret secret_key and handles the token exchange.

setupSteps.title: Create a Railz account
setupSteps.body: Sign up for the Railz Dashboard at https://dashboard.railz.ai/signup. Railz is now offered as FIS Accounting Data as a Service, but the dashboard and docs remain at the railz.ai domains.

setupSteps.title: Generate an API key
setupSteps.body: In the Dashboard left sidebar, go to Developers > API Keys and click Generate API Key. Enter a name and choose the appropriate permissions scope, then click Generate. The dashboard displays the Client ID and the Secret. Copy the secret immediately and store it securely, because it cannot be retrieved after you close the dialog. A team can have at most 5 API keys.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the Client ID into the client_id field and the secret into the secret_key field. Set start_date to the earliest date from which the connector should begin replicating data.
